cases.doc: Strategy's configured asset does not match the vault's asset.
cases.name: AssetMismatch

cases.doc: decimals_offset must be >= 3 to mitigate first-depositor inflation attacks.
cases.name: InvalidDecimalsOffset

cases.doc: A strategy's `get_balance()` returned a negative value.
cases.name: NegativeStrategyBalance

cases.doc: Cumulative AUM change within the current time window exceeds the limit.
cases.name: AumCumulativeChangeExceedsLimit

cases.doc:
A strategy's `get_balance()` call failed (panicked, returned a non-i128,
or otherwise could not be invoked). The vault freezes total-assets-dependent
operations until the admin removes the failing subaccount via
`remove_subaccount` (which does not make cross-contract calls).
cases.name: StrategyUnreachable

cases.doc: An operation specific to `Wallet` subaccounts was invoked against a\n`Strategy` subaccount (or vice versa). Distinct from `InvalidAmount`\nso monitoring can tell a type-mismatch apart from a bad numeric input.
cases.name: InvalidSubaccountType

cases.doc:
`withdraw_from_subaccount` attempted to pull more from a Wallet than
the per-wallet tracker records. Forces the operator to first reconcile
the wallet's attributed value via `update_wallet_deployed` (dust /
gain recognition) \xe2\x80\x94 otherwise an over-pull would silently consume
another wallet's share of `deployed_assets`, corrupting NAV.
cases.name: WalletOverWithdraw

cases.doc:
`remove_subaccount` was called on a subaccount whose
`WalletNetDeployed` tracker is non-zero. Primary case (Wallet):
removing without reconciliation would either strand value in
`deployed_assets` (if removed first) or open a pricing window
between write-down and removal (if reconciled separately).
Operators must either drain the wallet to zero via
`withdraw_from_subaccount` or use `remove_wallet_and_reconcile`,
which closes the window atomically under dual auth.

**Defense-in-depth case (Strategy)**: the post-R2 guard is
state-based, not type-based \xe2\x80\x94 it trips on any subaccount with a
non-zero tracker regardless of `SubaccountType`. A Strategy
subaccount never legitimately has a non-zero
`WalletNetDeployed`, so hitting this error on a Strategy
indicates storage corruption (stray tracker entry, wrong type
stored). The fix in that case is to investigate the
corruption, not to drain-then-remove.
cases.name: WalletTrackerNotZero

cases.doc:
`seed_wallet_net_deployed` was called with `amount < current_tracker`.
Seeding is an admin-only path that does not touch the aggregate
`deployed_assets`; allowing downward seeds would let an admin zero
a tracker to bypass `WalletTrackerNotZero` (#24) and then remove
the wallet, stranding value in the aggregate. Downward reconciliation
must go through `update_wallet_deployed` or
`remove_wallet_and_reconcile` (both move the aggregate in lockstep).
cases.name: WalletSeedBelowTracker

cases.doc:
`update_wallet_deployed_batch` was called with an empty `updates`
vector. Rejected for explicitness \xe2\x80\x94 a no-op batch call is almost
always a client-side bug (e.g. filtered list collapsed to empty)
that would otherwise silently succeed.
cases.name: EmptyBatch

cases.doc:
`update_wallet_deployed_batch` was called with the same subaccount
address appearing more than once in `updates`. Rejected because
the net-delta computation would otherwise depend on iteration
order (later pair overwrites earlier tracker value but the delta
applied to the aggregate sums all pairs), breaking the atomic
lockstep between per-wallet tracker and aggregate.
cases.name: DuplicateSubaccountInBatch

params.doc: The vault-wide `deployed_assets` counter at the time of removal.\nThis value is only meaningful for `Wallet`-type subaccounts;\n`Strategy` balances are tracked live via `get_balance()`.
params.name: total_deployed_assets

params.doc:
For `Strategy` subaccounts, the result of `try_get_balance()` at
removal time: `Some(balance)` if the strategy responded with a
valid i128, `None` if the call trapped or returned an undecodable
value (the vault still proceeds with removal to recover from a
broken strategy). Always `None` for `Wallet` subaccounts.

This is the on-chain record linking the share-price drop caused
by removing a funded strategy to the removal transaction itself \xe2\x80\x94
without it, off-chain auditors had no way to attribute the NAV
change to the removal event.
params.name: strategy_balance

params.doc: Amount the operator originally requested to transfer.
params.name: requested_amount

params.doc: Amount actually moved out of the vault, measured by balance-differencing.\nMay differ from `requested_amount` for fee-on-transfer or non-standard tokens.
params.name: actual_amount

udt_union_v0.doc:
Reason a strategy's `get_balance` probe failed at removal time. Split
from the unified `strategy_balance: None` encoding so off-chain
monitoring can distinguish "strategy panicked" (InvokeError \xe2\x80\x94 code
bug or missing entry point) from "strategy returned a non-i128"
(ConvertError \xe2\x80\x94 type-mismatch regression).
udt_union_v0.name: StrategyProbeFailure

void_v0.doc: The contract call itself failed (trap, missing function, host\nerror, TTL expiry, etc.).
void_v0.name: InvokeError

void_v0.doc: The call succeeded but the returned value could not be decoded\nas `i128`.
void_v0.name: ConvertError

event_v0.doc:
Emitted when a wallet's tracked net-deployed amount diverges from its
on-chain token balance at removal time. Informational only \xe2\x80\x94 the vault
does NOT use the tracker or the observed balance to adjust
`deployed_assets` on removal (that write-down is the admin's explicit
responsibility via `update_deployed_assets`). Indicates either external
dust sent to the wallet (tracked < balance) or an off-chain spend the
vault did not drive (tracked > balance).
event_v0.name: WalletBalanceDiverged

event_v0.doc:
Emitted when the operator reconciles a wallet's attributed value via
`update_wallet_deployed` \xe2\x80\x94 atomically moves both the per-wallet tracker
and the aggregate `deployed_assets` counter. `delta` is the signed
change applied to each (positive for recognised gains / dust recovery,
negative for losses). Distinct from `DeployedAssetsChanged`, which
signals an aggregate-only reconciliation (e.g. off-chain strategy AUM
not tied to a specific wallet).
event_v0.name: WalletDeployedUpdated

event_v0.doc:
Emitted when the admin seeds the per-wallet net-deployed tracker for a
Wallet subaccount. Used as a one-time migration step after an upgrade
that introduced the per-wallet tracker, to backfill accounting for
wallets that existed before the upgrade.
event_v0.name: WalletNetDeployedSeeded

event_v0.doc:
Emitted at wallet-removal time when the diagnostic `token.balance(wallet)`
probe fails (host-level invocation error or undecodable return value). The
removal itself succeeds \xe2\x80\x94 this event distinguishes "probe failed" from
"no divergence" for off-chain monitoring, so a silent skip of the
`WalletBalanceDiverged` diagnostic is still visible.
event_v0.name: WalletBalanceProbeFailed

event_v0.doc:
Emitted from `do_remove_subaccount` when `try_get_balance` cannot
produce an i128. Paired with `SubaccountRemoved`'s `strategy_balance:
None` but carries the distinguishing reason code so auditors can
differentiate the broken-strategy failure modes.

**Off-chain correlation**: the two events are emitted in the same
transaction and share the `subaccount` topic. Indexers rebuilding
the full picture should correlate by `(tx_hash, subaccount)`:
- `SubaccountRemoved` alone with `strategy_balance = Some(n)` \xe2\x86\x92
clean strategy removal, balance `n` exited NAV.
- `SubaccountRemoved` + `StrategyBalanceProbeFailed(InvokeError)` \xe2\x86\x92
strategy was unreachable (trap, missing method, TTL expiry).
- `SubaccountRemoved` + `StrategyBalanceProbeFailed(ConvertError)` \xe2\x86\x92
strategy responded but returned a non-i128; ABI regression.
- `SubaccountRemoved` alone with `strategy_balance = None` and
`subaccount_type = Wallet` \xe2\x86\x92 wallet removal, not a strategy
probe failure (the event is always `None` for wallets).
event_v0.name: StrategyBalanceProbeFailed

event_v0.doc:
Emitted once per `update_wallet_deployed_batch` call to record that a
group of per-wallet updates was applied as a single atomic unit.
Indexers use this as the transaction-level anchor for the N
`WalletDeployedUpdated` events that share the same transaction.

`count` is the number of entries in the batch. `net_delta` is the net
move applied to `deployed_assets` \xe2\x80\x94 equal to the sum of per-wallet
deltas. A zero `net_delta` is possible (e.g. one wallet gains 100k and
another loses 100k in the same batch) and still emits this event as
an audit trail.

**Emission order within the transaction** (indexers: scan backward
from this event to collect the batch members):

1. `DeployedAssetsChanged` \xe2\x80\x94 once, from `apply_deployed_assets_change`.
2. `WalletDeployedUpdated` \xe2\x80\x94 once per batch entry, in input order.
3. `WalletDeployedBatchApplied` \xe2\x80\x94 this event, emitted last.

The anchor fires last rather than first so that all per-wallet events
it summarises have already been emitted when an indexer sees it; the
cost is t
event_v0.name: WalletDeployedBatchApplied

void_v0.doc:
Map<Address, i128> \xe2\x80\x94 per-wallet net-deployed tracker.
Authoritative cap on wallet pulls: `withdraw_from_subaccount`
rejects any pull exceeding this value with `WalletOverWithdraw`.
Moved in lockstep with `deployed_assets` by
`update_wallet_deployed` when the operator recognises gains,
losses, or external dust. Also used by removal diagnostics
(emits `WalletBalanceDiverged` when tracked \xe2\x89\xa0 observed at
removal time).
void_v0.name: WalletNetDeployed

udt_union_v0.doc: Variant names must match the TypeScript `SubaccountType` union in\n`frontend/lib/subaccount-type.ts` \xe2\x80\x94 the frontend encodes these as\nSoroban symbols for `add_subaccount` calls.
udt_union_v0.name: SubaccountType

function_v0.doc:
Returns the name for this token.

# Arguments

* `e` - Access to Soroban environment.
function_v0.name: name

function_v0.doc:
Returns the symbol for this token.

# Arguments

* `e` - Access to Soroban environment.
function_v0.name: symbol

function_v0.doc:
Returns the amount of tokens held by `account`.

# Arguments

* `e` - Access to the Soroban environment.
* `account` - The address for which the balance is being queried.
function_v0.name: balance

function_v0.doc:
Returns the amount of tokens a `spender` is allowed to spend on behalf
of an `owner`.

# Arguments

* `e` - Access to Soroban environment.
* `owner` - The address holding the tokens.
* `spender` - The address authorized to spend the tokens.
function_v0.name: allowance

function_v0.doc:
Returns the address of the underlying asset that the vault manages.

# Arguments

* `e` - Access to the Soroban environment.

# Errors

* [`crate::vault::VaultTokenError::VaultAssetAddressNotSet`] - When the
vault's underlying asset address has not been initialized.
function_v0.name: query_asset

function_v0.doc: Accept admin role. Must be called by the address previously proposed\nvia `propose_admin` before the deadline expires. Clears the pending\nproposal.
function_v0.name: accept_admin

function_v0.doc: Note: setting admin == operator concentrates governance and AUM\nreporting under a single key. Prefer separate keys in production.
function_v0.name: set_operator

function_v0.doc:
Returns the total amount of tokens in circulation.

# Arguments

* `e` - Access to the Soroban environment.
function_v0.name: total_supply

function_v0.doc: Propose a new admin. The pending admin must call `accept_admin`\nbefore `deadline` (ledger timestamp) to complete the transfer.\nRevokes any previous pending proposal.
function_v0.name: propose_admin

function_v0.doc:
Register a new subaccount with the vault.

Requires admin authorization and a non-paused vault. For `Strategy`
subaccounts, performs interface validation: zero-amount smoke-tests
(`deposit`, `withdraw`), calls `get_balance()` (rejecting negative
values), and verifies the strategy's asset matches the vault's via
`get_asset()`. `Wallet` subaccounts skip all checks. Capped at
`MAX_SUBACCOUNTS` entries.
function_v0.name: add_subaccount

function_v0.doc: Returns the list of currently whitelisted subaccount addresses.
function_v0.name: get_subaccounts

function_v0.doc:
Removes a subaccount from the vault's whitelist. Whitelist-and-
tracker cleanup only \xe2\x80\x94 does NOT adjust `deployed_assets`.

For Wallet subaccounts with non-zero attributed value, use
`remove_wallet_and_reconcile`: it does the aggregate write-down and
the removal atomically under dual auth, closing the pricing window
of the two-step sequence (`update_deployed_assets` +
`remove_subaccount`) where NAV is wrong between transactions.
`remove_subaccount` rejects Wallet subaccounts whose
`WalletNetDeployed` tracker is non-zero (`WalletTrackerNotZero`) to
prevent that exact window being opened by mistake.

Remaining use cases for `remove_subaccount`:
- Strategy removal (balances tracked live via `get_balance()`).
- Wallet cleanup after a full pull via `withdraw_from_subaccount`
has zeroed the tracker.
- Emergency decommissioning where the operator key is unavailable:
the admin must first rotate the operator (via
`set_operator`) and then go through `remove_wallet_and_reconcile`
or `update_wallet_deployed` to zero the
function_v0.name: remove_subaccount

function_v0.doc:
Returns the type of a registered subaccount (`Strategy` or `Wallet`).

Panics with `SubaccountNotWhitelisted` if the address is not in the
current whitelist. Defaults to `Strategy` for subaccounts registered
before this field was introduced (backward compatibility).
function_v0.name: get_subaccount_type

function_v0.doc: Cancel a pending admin transfer proposal. Only the current admin\ncan cancel.
function_v0.name: cancel_admin_proposal

function_v0.doc:
Transfer `amount` of the underlying token from the vault to `subaccount`.

For `Strategy` subaccounts, notifies the strategy via `IStrategy::deposit`
after the transfer. The strategy's balance is captured live by
`IStrategy::get_balance()` in `total_assets`, so `deployed_assets` is NOT
incremented. `Wallet` subaccounts receive the tokens directly with no
further notification; their balance is not queried on-chain, so
`deployed_assets` IS incremented. No AUM rate limit is applied \xe2\x80\x94
subaccount transfers don't change total_assets.

Requires operator authorization, a non-paused vault, and a whitelisted
subaccount.
function_v0.name: deposit_to_subaccount

function_v0.doc: Sum of `IStrategy::get_balance()` across all Strategy-type subaccounts.\nUseful for operators to see how much capital is visible on-chain in\nstrategies vs. the wallet-attributed `deployed_assets`.
function_v0.name: get_strategy_balances

function_v0.doc:
Configure the cumulative AUM rate-limit window.

`window_duration` \xe2\x80\x94 length of the fixed window in seconds
(min 3,600 = 1 hour, max 604,800 = 7 days). The window resets
entirely on expiry (tumbling window, not sliding).

`cumulative_increase_bps` / `cumulative_decrease_bps` \xe2\x80\x94 maximum
cumulative change (in basis points of the `base_deployed` snapshot
taken when the window resets) allowed within a single window.

Changing limits resets the active window so the new configuration
takes effect immediately with a clean cumulative state.
function_v0.name: set_aum_window_limits

function_v0.doc:
Aggregate-only emergency escape hatch for `deployed_assets`.

**Deprecated for routine use** after F4: strategies self-report
their full position (idle + deployed) via `IStrategy::get_balance()`,
so `deployed_assets` no longer has a legitimate "strategy off-chain
AUM" component. The standard operator path for wallet-attributed
reconciliation is `update_wallet_deployed` (single wallet) or
`update_wallet_deployed_batch` (multiple wallets atomically) \xe2\x80\x94 both
move the per-wallet tracker and the aggregate in lockstep, so the
invariant `\xce\xa3 WalletNetDeployed == deployed_assets` is preserved.

Remaining legitimate uses:
- Recovery from a storage anomaly where the invariant has already
broken and the aggregate needs a correction that cannot be
attributed to specific wallets.
- Temporary aggregate correction during a multi-step migration that
will restore the invariant in a follow-up transaction.

**Warning**: this function can break the wallet-tracker sum
invariant by design. Off-chain monitoring should compare
`get_
function_v0.name: update_deployed_assets

function_v0.doc:
Reconcile a single Wallet subaccount's attributed value. Atomically
moves both the per-wallet tracker (to `new_tracked`) and the
aggregate `deployed_assets` counter (by the same delta), so
subsequent `withdraw_from_subaccount` calls can pull the full value
without tripping the per-wallet over-pull check.

Use cases:
- Recognising a gain (wallet appreciated): `new_tracked > current`.
- Recognising a loss (wallet depreciated): `new_tracked < current`.
- Recognising external dust the operator wants to recover:
`new_tracked = current + dust_amount`, then pull.

Subject to the same AUM rate limits and pause semantics as
`update_deployed_assets`: increases are rejected while paused;
decreases remain allowed for loss recovery.

For aggregate-only reconciliation (e.g. strategy external AUM not
attributable to a specific wallet) use `update_deployed_assets`
instead.
function_v0.name: update_wallet_deployed

function_v0.doc: Length of the cumulative AUM rate-limit window in seconds\n(default: 86,400 = 24 hours).
function_v0.name: get_aum_window_duration

function_v0.doc:
Read the per-wallet net-deployed tracker for a subaccount.

Returns 0 for unregistered addresses, for Strategy subaccounts, and
for Wallet subaccounts that have never been deposited to. Useful for
admins verifying the result of `seed_wallet_net_deployed` and for
monitoring dashboards.
function_v0.name: get_wallet_net_deployed

function_v0.doc: Maximum cumulative AUM decrease within a window, in basis points\n(default: 500 = 5%).
function_v0.name: get_aum_window_dec_limit

function_v0.doc: Maximum cumulative AUM increase within a window, in basis points\n(default: 1,000 = 10%).
function_v0.name: get_aum_window_inc_limit

function_v0.doc:
Per-wallet emergency escape hatch for the `WalletNetDeployed`
tracker. **Restricted to emergency storage corrections** after F4:
the standard operator path for gain/loss recognition and dust
attribution is `update_wallet_deployed` (single wallet) or
`update_wallet_deployed_batch` (multiple wallets atomically),
which move the per-wallet tracker and the aggregate
`deployed_assets` in lockstep and preserve the invariant
`\xce\xa3 WalletNetDeployed == deployed_assets`.

Remaining legitimate uses:
- Backfilling the tracker for a wallet registered before per-wallet
accounting was introduced (one-shot upgrade migration).
- Correcting a divergence between `get_deployed_assets()` and
`get_wallet_deployed_assets()` after a storage anomaly or a prior
`update_deployed_assets` correction that left wallet attribution
out of sync.

**Warning**: this function can break the wallet-tracker sum
invariant by design. It does NOT touch the aggregate
`deployed_assets` \xe2\x80\x94 use it only when the value being attributed is
already recorded in
function_v0.name: seed_wallet_net_deployed

function_v0.doc:
Request `amount` of the underlying token back from `subaccount`.

For `Strategy` subaccounts, calls `IStrategy::withdraw` (push model).
Strategy balances are queried live via `get_balance()`, so
`deployed_assets` is NOT decremented.

For `Wallet` subaccounts, pulls tokens via `transfer_from` using a
pre-approved SEP-41 allowance. The wallet owner (EOA or contract) must
have called `token.approve(wallet, vault, amount, ledger)` beforehand.
`deployed_assets` IS decremented for wallets.

Measures actual tokens received via balance-differencing. No AUM rate
limit is applied \xe2\x80\x94 subaccount transfers don't change total_assets.

Requires operator authorization, a non-paused vault, and a whitelisted
subaccount.
function_v0.name: withdraw_from_subaccount

function_v0.doc:
Sum of `WalletNetDeployed` across all Wallet-type subaccounts
currently in the whitelist.

Exposes the \xce\xa3 side of the F4 invariant
`\xce\xa3 WalletNetDeployed == deployed_assets` for off-chain monitoring.
Under normal operation this returns the same value as
`get_deployed_assets()`. Divergence indicates one of the
documented escape hatches (`update_deployed_assets` or
`seed_wallet_net_deployed`) has been used; the gap represents
aggregate value not currently attributed to any wallet.

Bounded cost: the subaccount list is capped at `MAX_SUBACCOUNTS`
(= 10), so this iterates at most ten map lookups.
function_v0.name: get_wallet_deployed_assets

function_v0.doc:
Atomically reconcile the aggregate `deployed_assets` counter AND
remove a Wallet subaccount in a single transaction.

Closes the pricing window between the two-step sequence:
1. `update_deployed_assets(operator, new_total)`
2. `remove_subaccount(admin, wallet)`

Between those transactions NAV is wrong in one direction or the
other (write-down first: NAV under-reports while tokens still sit
at the wallet \xe2\x86\x92 deposits mint too many shares; remove first: NAV
over-reports against stranded value \xe2\x86\x92 redeems overpay). Combining
both into one call eliminates the window.

Requires both `admin` (whitelist authority) and `operator` (AUM
authority) to authorise \xe2\x80\x94 the combined function unions their
permissions without granting either role unilateral access to the
other's powers. When a single entity holds both keys (documented
supported configuration on `set_operator`), the operator's
`require_auth` is skipped to avoid Soroban's `Auth, ExistingValue`
error that otherwise fires on a repeated auth of the same address.
Su
function_v0.name: remove_wallet_and_reconcile

function_v0.doc:
Reconcile multiple Wallet subaccounts atomically in a single call.
Introduced as the F4 primary operator path for wallet-attributed
reconciliation: replaces ad-hoc sequences of `update_wallet_deployed`
calls with one transaction that moves the aggregate exactly once
(so the AUM rate limit is applied to the net delta rather than to
each intermediate step).

Each `(subaccount, new_tracked)` entry is validated independently
(whitelisted, Wallet type, non-negative tracker). The net delta
`\xce\xa3(new_tracked - old_tracked)` is applied to `deployed_assets` via
the shared `apply_deployed_assets_change` helper, so the AUM rate
limit is measured against the net (a batch with +100k on wallet A
and -100k on wallet B passes even when each individual step would
exceed the limit). Pause is enforced **per entry**: while paused,
any positive entry delta panics with `VaultPaused` regardless of
the batch's net \xe2\x80\x94 this matches `update_wallet_deployed` (where
per-entry and net deltas coincide) and prevents a paused vault
from accep
function_v0.name: update_wallet_deployed_batch

udt_error_enum_v0.doc: Errors that can occur in timelock operations.
udt_error_enum_v0.name: TimelockError

cases.doc: The operation is already scheduled
cases.name: OperationAlreadyScheduled

cases.doc: The delay is less than the minimum required delay
cases.name: InsufficientDelay

cases.doc: The operation is not in the expected state
cases.name: InvalidOperationState

cases.doc: A predecessor operation has not been executed yet
cases.name: UnexecutedPredecessor

cases.doc: The caller is not authorized to perform this action
cases.name: Unauthorized

cases.doc: The minimum delay has not been set
cases.name: MinDelayNotSet

cases.doc: The operation has not been scheduled
cases.name: OperationNotScheduled

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when the minimum delay is changed.
event_v0.name: MinDelayChanged

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when an operation is executed.
event_v0.name: OperationExecuted

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when an operation is cancelled.
event_v0.name: OperationCancelled

event_v0.doc: Event emitted when an operation is scheduled.
event_v0.name: OperationScheduled

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Represents a operation to be executed by the timelock.

An operation encapsulates all the information needed to invoke a function
on a target contract after the timelock delay has passed.
udt_struct_v0.name: Operation

fields.doc: The serialized arguments to pass to the function
fields.name: args

fields.doc: The function name to invoke on the target contract
fields.name: function

fields.doc: Hash of a predecessor operation that must be executed first.\nUse BytesN::<32>::from_array(&[0u8; 32]) for no predecessor.
fields.name: predecessor

fields.doc: A salt value for operation uniqueness.\nAllows scheduling the same operation multiple times with different IDs.
fields.name: salt

udt_union_v0.doc: The state of an operation in the timelock system.
udt_union_v0.name: OperationState

void_v0.doc: Operation has not been scheduled
void_v0.name: Unset

void_v0.doc: Operation is scheduled but the delay period has not passed
void_v0.name: Waiting

void_v0.doc: Operation is ready to be executed (delay has passed)
void_v0.name: Ready

void_v0.doc: Operation has been executed
void_v0.name: Done

udt_union_v0.doc: Storage keys for the timelock module.
udt_union_v0.name: TimelockStorageKey

void_v0.doc: Minimum delay in seconds for operations
void_v0.name: MinDelay

tuple_v0.doc: Maps operation ID to the timestamp when it will be in a\n[`OperationState::Ready`] state (Note: value is 0 for\n[`OperationState::Unset`], 1 for [`OperationState:Done`]).
tuple_v0.name: Timestamp

event_v0.name: ModuleRemoved

udt_union_v0.doc:
Hook types for modular compliance system.

Each hook type represents a specific event or validation point
where compliance modules can be executed.
udt_union_v0.name: ComplianceHook

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully transferred from one wallet to\nanother. Modules registered for this hook can update their state\nbased on transfer events.
void_v0.name: Transferred

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully created/minted to a wallet.\nModules registered for this hook can update their state based on minting\nevents.
void_v0.name: Created

void_v0.doc: Called after tokens are successfully destroyed/burned from a wallet.\nModules registered for this hook can update their state based on burning\nevents.
void_v0.name: Destroyed

void_v0.doc:
Called during transfer validation to check if a transfer should be
allowed. Modules registered for this hook can implement transfer
restrictions. This is a READ-only operation and should not modify
state.
void_v0.name: CanTransfer

void_v0.doc:
Called during mint validation to check if a mint operation should be
allowed. Modules registered for this hook can implement transfer
restrictions. This is a READ-only operation and should not modify
state.
void_v0.name: CanCreate

cases.doc: Indicates a module is already registered for this hook.
cases.name: ModuleAlreadyRegistered

cases.doc: Indicates a module is not registered for this hook.
cases.name: ModuleNotRegistered

cases.doc: Indicates a module bound is exceeded.
cases.name: ModuleBoundExceeded

cases.doc: Indicates a token is not bound to this compliance contract.
cases.name: TokenNotBound

udt_union_v0.doc: Storage keys for the modular compliance contract.
udt_union_v0.name: ComplianceDataKey

tuple_v0.doc: Maps ComplianceHook -> `Vec<Address>` for registered modules
tuple_v0.name: HookModules

udt_union_v0.doc:
Storage keys for the token binder system.

- Tokens are stored in buckets of 100 addresses each
- Each bucket is a `Vec<Address>` stored under its bucket index
- Total count is tracked separately
- When a token is unbound, the last token is moved to fill the gap
(swap-remove pattern)
udt_union_v0.name: TokenBinderStorageKey

tuple_v0.doc: Maps bucket index to a vector of token addresses (max 100 per bucket)
tuple_v0.name: TokenBucket

void_v0.doc: Total count of bound tokens
